key_points: Summer subleasing is nearly impossible; market completely dead May-July
Off-campus Orlando housing runs $700+/month, tight supply near campus
No dorm availability reported for some transfer students
key_quotes: Yeah I'm trying to sublease for June to July and offered literally half off the rent. Nobody cares
Transfer student admitted to UCF, However, no dorms available
Off campus housing in Tampa is great, I can get a really nice place for less than $650 vs Orlando where I have to pay $700+

key_points: Massive diversity of student groups—furries, yaoi clubs, sororities all coexist
Making friends is genuinely hard; club friend groups are already established
Older students (25+) feel like aliens on a campus dominated by 18-year-olds
key_quotes: Everyone says 'join a club' but nobody ever talks about how when you join a club they always have a pre established friend group
That's my favorite thing about UCF it's so big that you can find every kind of group here
I'm 25. I really wish there was a better group for people 22 and up at ucf. I feel like a fucking alien

key_points: Student Union food options exist but fire incidents at Panda Express reported
Gas leaks frequently force Student Union evacuations mid-meal
No strong praise for dining quality in any posts reviewed
key_quotes: Thank god I got my lunch right before they had us evacuated
I WAS JUST THERE EATING PANDA HELP???????
every fucking month bro — Student Union gas leak post

key_points: Students report anxiety relief after choosing UCF over prestige-chasing
Staff openly breaking down in cars over no raises and RTO mandates
Campus threats and shooter scares create recurring stress cycles
key_quotes: I'm so incredibly happy here. I'm 100x healthier. My anxiety has decreased dramatically
Considering how I broke down in my car two days ago, this is Fantastic advice — reacting to UCF's no-raise motivational poster
I know this is jokes but it's scary to think how this school and professors didn't take it seriously

key_points: National Merit Scholars get full rides plus laptop and scheduling perks
Bright Futures covers significant costs for qualifying Florida residents
Benacquisto Scholarship eliminated for classes after 2025—huge loss for NMF students
key_quotes: UCF gives a few more benefits such as a free laptop and internship and scheduling priorities
The Benacquisto Scholarship was just ended. FUCK YOU FLORIDA LEGISLATURE
I get no financial aid since my EFC is too high... my father can no longer afford to pay my tuition

key_points: CS Foundation Exam is a brutal gatekeeping hurdle unique to UCF
Specific professors like Khondaker infamous for 30% midterm averages
Flipped classroom format widely hated; honors college praised for smaller classes
key_quotes: I'm in Khondaker's class as well. He sucks at teaching full stop. Averages on his midterms are in the 30%'s
He was a terrible professor 10-15 years ago as well. Glad to see UCF has done nothing with those student surveys

I spent the summer interning in Colorado and because orientation was in person I could not attend... leaving me few choices. They are all flipped classroom meeting only 6 times the whole semester which fucking sucks

key_points: President Cartwright took 60% raise while giving faculty and staff 0% raise
Admissions office is chaotic—decisions range from 2 days to 3 months
AI-generated presidential emails sent to students; staff layoffs ongoing
key_quotes: Cartwright receives 60% raise this year while offering faculty 0%
UCF's admissions office is a shit show... it could take anywhere from 2 days to 3 months to get your decision
I started this school really proud and excited to be here and have more often than not been left disappointed

key_points: Campus is enormous—visible from airplanes, walkable shortcuts blocked by administration
Gas leaks in Student Union happen nearly every month with no permanent fix
Orlando location gives access to NASA, theme parks, and aerospace industry
key_quotes: Surely 69,000 students wouldn't be able to comfortably attend a university that size
They will never actually fix it — on recurring gas leaks
Top left of the first picture, you can see NASA's vehicle assembly building at KSC on the coast. Pretty cool!

key_points: Strong aerospace and engineering pipeline to NASA, Lockheed Martin nearby
HSA internship requirement nearly blocked one student from graduating after 70+ rejections
CS and engineering grads report multiple job offers; proximity to tech corridor helps
key_quotes: Congratulations! My husband went to UCF and works at Lockheed
I FUCKING hate UCF. They have an internship requirement for HSA majors. After applying to no less than 70 internships last semester, I never heard back
multiple job/internship offers — UCF CS student on r/ApplyingToCollege

key_points: In-state tuition plus Bright Futures makes UCF one of Florida's best value options
National Merit full rides with laptop and perks make it genuinely elite value
Out-of-state students get little aid; athletic fees mandatory but parking costs extra
key_quotes: It makes me mad that athletic fees are included in my tuition, but parking is not
I get no financial aid since my EFC is too high
Full ride! Congrats!!! Now take advantage of them — reaction to NMF student's UCF acceptance

key_points: Chaotic, weird, and weirdly lovable—gas leaks, furries, communal urinals are UCF lore
Students defend UCF fiercely despite its dysfunction; strong pride after graduation
School feels like a spreadsheet bureaucracy to many but a home to those who find their niche
key_quotes: Obviously they put something in the gas leaks to make this such a magical place
I mean this university is basically just a spreadsheet in a system on how they treat the students
It still feels so surreal that I just graduated. It's been such an amazing experience

key_points: **Administration:** President took 60% raise; faculty got 0%; staff burning out under RTO
**Safety:** Recurring campus threats not taken seriously by some professors; shooter scares multiple times
**Parking:** 45-minute hunts, barnacle boots, app lies about availability—systemic and unfixed
key_quotes: If you're in leadership in a non revenue business you shouldn't take a raise if your staff gets no raises. He is paid extremely well prior to the 60% increase
He's a freaking CS professor. Record the lecture and post it for those who avoided campus. Why is common sense so uncommon? — on professor dismissing a shooter threat
The app straight up lies about occupancy now. I garage is currently listed at 0% full, yet it's 100%

key_points: Student-built tools like ucfSpots and feprep.net fill gaps admin ignores
Honors College gives priority registration, smaller classes, and scholarship access
Memory Mall events and spontaneous campus art make UCF unexpectedly charming
key_quotes: I built ucfSpots, a real-time map that shows which UCF classrooms are actually empty right now
The honors college still has tons of exceptionally bright, motivated students. The school still has vast resources and the top students get the lion's share
We finally found them! If I knew who they were, I would tip them. Because I love seeing their art all over campus when I least expect it
